title: Change API socket to be machine name isolated
body:
Fixes  #21899

Change API socket to be machine name isolated

- Fixes conflicts such as removal of second machine deleting a socket of a the first machine while it's running
- Move API socket into runtime directory for consistency
- Add API and gvproxy sockets to removal list
- Cleanup related logic

```release-note
Fixes a problem where podman machine rm removes a socket used by other machines - #21899
```

comments.body:
This needs to be addressed in the Podman Desktop configuration, so that it can find the "new" socket.

https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/blob/main/extensions/podman/src/extension.ts

```typescript
  let socketPath = path.resolve(podmanMachineSocketsDirectory, machineName, 'podman.sock');
  if (isMovedPodmanSocket) {
    socketPath = path.resolve(podmanMachineSocketsDirectory, 'qemu', 'podman.sock');
  }
```

Ideally the app would allow the user to configure the socket location, or to support ssh sockets.

EDIT: Seems like it does do the podman machine inspect first now, so this was just the fallback....

```typescript
            'machine',
            'inspect',
            '--format',
            '{{.ConnectionInfo.PodmanSocket.Path}}',
            machineName,
```

Should probably only do this for 5.0, to make it easier to "know" when the breaking change occurs.

The previous breakpoint was 4.5, when the machine name was changed into the provider name...

Nobody should need to "guess" the socket location, the location is printed on machine start and part of podman machine inspect so users should get it from there and not rely on a fixed path in general.
LGTM, but I'd prefer to have an answer on the MacOS thing before we merge. Is it a socket path-length restriction?
Will push a change to address two points in next few mins
